Step 1: Identify the data points from the stem and leaf plot.
The data points are: 12, 13, 14, 15, 15, 22, 22, 22, 31, 31, 38, 39, 40, 41, 42, 43, 52, 58, 59.

Step 2: Determine the median.
The median is the middle value of the data set when it is ordered. Since there are 19 data points, the median is the 10th value.
The ordered data points are: 12, 13, 14, 15, 15, 22, 22, 22, 31, 31, 38, 39, 40, 41, 42, 43, 52, 58, 59.
The 10th value is 31.
So, the median is 31.

Step 3: Determine the mode.
The mode is the value that appears most frequently in the data set.
From the data points, we can see that the value 22 appears three times, which is more frequent than any other value.
So, the mode is 22.

Step 4: Calculate the positive difference between the median and the mode.
The positive difference between the median (31) and the mode (22) is:
\[ 31 - 22 = 9 \]

So, the positive difference between the median and the mode is \(\boxed{9}\).

Step 4: Determine the mode.
The mode is the value that appears most frequently in the data set. From the ordered list, we can see that the value 22 appears three times, which is more frequent than any other value. So, the mode is 22.
